target.name: Parallel Systems
target.founder: Matt Soule, John Howard, Ben Stabler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $2.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Parallel Systems is a Series B transportation company that has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies in the transportation sector at similar and adjacent stages; no public comparables exist in this frontier subsector. Key peers include Einride ($1.8B, Series C), Harbinger ($500M, Series C), and REGENT Craft ($270M, Series A).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $100M raised as a proxy for a $2.0B earlier valuation, then applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, yielding $3.8B. Peer-median analysis across the three comparable companies produces a median valuation of $500M, with a range of $270M to $1.8B. The wide dispersion reflects stage and business model variance within the transportation peer set.

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $425M (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The substantial gap between the peer-median and stage-progression methods reflects uncertainty around how much capital deployment and progress at Parallel Systems justifies relative to the peer cohort. Key sensitivities include the applicability of stage-step multiples in an early-stage, capital-intensive sector, and whether peer valuations adequately reflect execution risk specific to frontier transportation technologies.
